description: A heap use-after-free vulnerability exists in V8's Atomics.Mutex async unlock path. When `Atomics.Mutex.lockAsync` is called, a `LockAsyncWaiterQueueNode` (a C++ object managed by a `unique_ptr` in the isolate's `async_waiter_queue_nodes` list) is created and its raw pointer is wrapped in a `Foreign` object stored in a promise handler context at `kAsyncLockedWaiterAsyncContextSlot`. An attacker can forge two PromiseReaction/Context objects (e.g., by copying the Foreign field between contexts) so that they both hold a pointer to the same underlying `LockAsyncWaiterQueueNode`. When the first reaction's async unlock handler fires, `UnlockAsyncLockedMutex` calls `RemoveFromAsyncWaiterQueueList`, which removes and destroys the node's `unique_ptr`, freeing the C++ object. When the second reaction fires, `RemoveFromAsyncWaiterQueueList` is called again with the same stale pointer; it dereferences the freed memory to read `node->requester_` (to access the isolate's list), causing a use-after-free read. The `remove_if` call then performs list operations on the stale pointer, extending the UAF access.

fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/6929545 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
